Idea Generation.definition: The writer gets their ideas down on the page.
verbalizer: Jot down raw ideas related to the project title
Write phrases or sentences about project

Idea Organization.definition: The writer is structuring the concepts, arguments, and topics that they produced during the idea generation process.
verbalizer: Organize ideas in paper by rearranging sentences
Structure the paper by rearranging sentences

Discourse Planning.definition: The writer is planning how to organize and relate their concepts to each other. This should be also written in an incomplete format.
verbalizer: Create new sections on the paper

Drafting.definition: The writer completes their raw ideas into full sentences.
verbalizer: Write full sentences about the topic.
Write body of paper in full sentences

Lexical Chaining.definition: when a sequence of sentences is linked by the semantic relationships between the words in the sentences.
verbalizer: Use transition words to structure sentences within a paragraph
Write down a new sentence that continues explaining the following sentence.

Object Insertion.definition: This is an activity of inserting figures, tables, equations, footnotes, etc. to explain and support certain parts of their writing.

Command Insertion.definition: Adding some relevant packages and/or macros to implement figures is applied to this activity.
verbalizer: Add relevant latex packages and commands

Citation.definition: An activity of inserting citations to support their claim with evidence.
verbalizer: Add a citation on the paper
Add a citation to the document

Syntactic.definition: Syntactic revisions fix grammar, spelling, and punctuation
verbalizer: Fix the grammar in this file
Fix the grammar in this paper
Improve the grammar in this paper

Lexical.definition: Lexical revisions change words to clarify meaning or improve coherence.
verbalizer: Improve the coherence of the paper
Make lexical revisions to improve the coherence of the paper

Semantic.definition: Semantic revisions add more clear meaning to a written sentence to have better elaboration or /explanations (refined meaning), rather than simply altering the words themselves.
verbalizer: Clarify the meaning of the paper
Add or remove examples to clarify the meaning of the text

Structural.definition: Structural revisions move text or figures or change paragraph boundaries to improve the organization of the paper.

Visual.definition: Change visualization of texts or improve the figures and tables, etc. that are the objects. Citations can be included as visual
verbalizer: Improve the look of the document
Make changes to the style of the documents including changing font weight, moving around figures, and changing the style of lists

Quantitative.definition: Change the numeric results to quantitatively support their claims.

Feedback.definition: Provide feedback to question or improve the draft, which prompts the revision action.
